title: Sweep a Hard Floor
summary: Sweep a hard floor to remove dust, crumbs, and debris.
learnerPromise: You will be able to sweep a hard floor clean and collect the debris into a dustpan.
whyItMatters: Regular sweeping keeps floors safe to walk on and prevents dirt from spreading to other rooms. It is one of the fastest ways to refresh a space.
realLifeUses: Cleaning up crumbs after cooking or eating
Removing dust and dirt tracked in from outside
result: A swept floor with visible debris collected and disposed of.
youWillLearn: How to hold a broom at the right angle for effective sweeping
How to sweep debris into a pile without scattering it
How to use a dustpan to collect a pile cleanly
How to reach under furniture and into corners
miniChallenge: Sweep one room with a hard floor today. Collect the debris and throw it away.
steps: Clear small items off the floor so nothing blocks your path.
Hold the broom with both hands and start sweeping from the edges of the room inward.
Use short, controlled strokes to push dust and debris into a central pile.
Position the dustpan flat on the floor and sweep the pile into it.
Empty the dustpan into a trash bin and put the broom away.
completionCriteria: Floor is visibly free of dust, crumbs, and loose debris
Debris has been collected and disposed of in a trash bin
commonProblems: Dust line left at dustpan edge — tilt the dustpan slightly back and sweep again
Debris scatters — use lighter strokes and sweep toward you, not away
Hair sticks to broom — remove it with a paper towel or old cloth after sweeping
tips: Sweep toward a light source so you can see dust better
Work from the far corner toward the door so you do not walk over what you cleaned
For pet hair, slightly dampen the broom bristles to help it clump together
